rationale: Unprovable from the given spec. `CSingleDelivery::receive_ack_impl` is `#[verifier::external_body]` and its ensures only constrains the abstract view via `SingleDelivery::receive_ack(pre@, post@, pkt@, set!{})`. The synthetic determinism conclusion `det_receive_ack_impl_equal` requires concrete equality on `post.receive_state.epmap` and `post.send_state.epmap`, which are `HashMap<V>` fields. `HashMap` is `#[verifier(external_body)]` with `uninterp spec fn view`, so there is no axiom (no injectivity, no view-to-concrete bridge) linking abstract-view equality to concrete `==`. The abstract-level case split (then-branch: send_state determined; else-branch: post == pre) is fully provable via `=~=` on Map, but cannot be lifted to concrete `epmap == epmap`. The previous attempt left the same residual two failures, confirming the obstruction is structural rather than tactical. No legal proof tactic (no assume / admit / external_body allowed) can close this synthetic check.
